title: Navarretia rubra
taxonomy: Navarretia rubra is classified within the family Polemoniaceae, commonly known as the globe mallow family. This family consists of approximately 25 genera and 250 species. Navarretia rubra itself is a genus of annual or biennial plants, primarily found in North America. The species is further classified within the genus Navarretia, which includes around 20 species. The classification of Navarretia rubra is also related to the larger classification of flowering plants, specifically within the clade Angiosperms.

However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Navarretia rubra for medicinal purposes. Some studies suggest that it may have potential in treating certain types of infections and promoting wound healing.

intro: Navarretia rubra, also known as Red Platte Lake Navarretia, is a flowering plant species in the phlox family, characterized by its bright red flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Navarretia rubra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Navarretia rubra include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional folk medicine to treat colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions.
